mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signal supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases does not predict negative 5-day forward returns, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates monetary tightening that increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.2% alongside non-declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, providing a fundamental earnings tailwind that generates positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts decline exceeding 10% signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a HOUST drop greater than 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing payroll growth during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capital-intensive productivity gains that boost corporate margins and drive positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS growth differential fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in post-shock periods, with a directional accuracy below 50%.
